created_from: terminal prompt: conformal U(1) BSM extension with dark scalar, dark gauge boson, dark fermion
approximations: Higgs portal coupling set to zero (lambda_HPhi = 0)
Gauge kinetic mixing set to zero (epsilon = 0)
Dark sector thermalised with SM (xi = T_dark/T_SM = 1)
High-temperature expansion of finite-T effective potential valid in supercooling regime
Gaussian approximation for false vacuum fraction

gauge_groups.name: U(1)_D
gauge_groups.notes: Dark U(1) gauge symmetry; anomaly-free with the chosen fermion content

global_symmetries.name: Classical scale invariance
global_symmetries.notes: No dimensionful parameters in tree-level Lagrangian; broken radiatively by CW mechanism

particle_content.name: Phi
particle_content.notes: Dark Higgs; radial mode acquires vev triggering U(1)_D SSB

particle_content.name: Aprime
particle_content.notes: Dark photon; gets mass g_D v via Higgs mechanism

particle_content.name: psi_1
particle_content.notes: First dark Dirac fermion; vector-like (L and R same charge); anomaly-free

particle_content.name: psi_2
particle_content.notes: Second dark Dirac fermion; vector-like (L and R same charge); anomaly-free

lagrangian_terms.name: gauge_kinetic
lagrangian_terms.notes: Dark U(1)_D gauge kinetic term; no kinetic mixing with SM U(1)_Y at tree level

lagrangian_terms.name: scalar_kinetic
lagrangian_terms.notes: Kinetic term for complex scalar; D_mu = d_mu + i * g_D * A'_mu

lagrangian_terms.name: fermion_kinetic_psi1
lagrangian_terms.notes: Kinetic term for psi_1; D_mu = d_mu + i * g_D * (1/2) * A'_mu

lagrangian_terms.name: fermion_kinetic_psi2
lagrangian_terms.notes: Kinetic term for psi_2; D_mu = d_mu - i * g_D * (1/2) * A'_mu

lagrangian_terms.name: yukawa
lagrangian_terms.notes: Renormalizable gauge-invariant Yukawa coupling; after SSB generates Dirac mass m_psi = y_psi * v / sqrt(2)

lagrangian_terms.name: scalar_potential
lagrangian_terms.notes: Tree-level conformal scalar potential; no mass term; lambda_Phi fixed by CW condition

free_parameters.name: g_D
free_parameters.notes: Dark U(1)_D gauge coupling; perturbativity requires g_D < 4*pi

free_parameters.name: y_psi
free_parameters.notes: Yukawa coupling; CW condition requires y_psi < (3/2)^(1/4) * g_D approx 1.11 * g_D

free_parameters.name: v
free_parameters.notes: Dark Higgs vev; sets the overall mass scale of the dark sector

g_D.notes: Dark gauge coupling; scanned in MCMC

y_psi.notes: Yukawa coupling; scanned in MCMC; constrained by CW condition beta_lambda > 0

v.notes: Dark Higgs vev; scanned in MCMC

lambda_Phi.notes: CW-fixed quartic coupling; NOT scanned; beta_lambda > 0 required for radiative SSB

m_chi.notes: Physical mass of the CW pseudo-dilaton (radial scalar mode)

m_psi.notes: Mass of each Dirac fermion mass eigenstate from psi_1-psi_2 mixing

gb_scalar.notes: Scalar self-coupling gb for backend; fixed by particle content; subleading in potential

xi.notes: T_dark / T_SM temperature ratio; assumes dark sector thermalised with SM

epsilon_kinetic_mixing.notes: Gauge kinetic mixing between U(1)_D and U(1)_Y; set to zero at tree level

lambda_HPhi.notes: Higgs portal coupling |H|^2|Phi|^2; set to zero (no tree-level mixing)

ssb_pattern: U(1)_D -> nothing; complex scalar Phi acquires vev v, giving masses to gauge boson and fermions
vev_convention: Phi = (chi + v) / sqrt(2) * exp(I * theta / v) in unitary gauge; radial mode chi is the background field with <chi> = v

tree_level_potential.name: V_tree
tree_level_potential.notes: Tree-level scalar potential in radial background field chi; classically scale-invariant

field_dependent_masses.notes: Dark gauge boson transverse modes; gets mass from covariant derivative of Phi
Radial scalar mode; mass from CW potential; subleading in FOPT dynamics (gb=0 in backend)
Two Dirac fermion mass eigenstates from psi_1-psi_2 mixing; each with m^2 = y_psi^2 * chi^2 / 2

thermal_masses.notes: Debye/thermal mass squared for longitudinal gauge boson mode; g2sq = 3*g_D^2 + 2*y_psi^2 in backend notation
Thermal mass squared for radial scalar; same g2sq coefficient as gauge boson
Thermal mass for Goldstone mode (eaten by A'_mu); contributes to thermal barrier

assumptions: Two Dirac fermions (psi_1, psi_2) are required because a single Dirac fermion cannot simultaneously satisfy gauge invariance of the Yukawa term, anomaly cancellation, and non-zero scalar charge under U(1)_D
The Higgs portal coupling lambda_HPhi is set to zero; no tree-level mixing between SM and dark scalar sectors
Gauge kinetic mixing epsilon between U(1)_D and U(1)_Y is set to zero at all scales
Dark sector is assumed to be in thermal equilibrium with the SM (xi = T_dark/T_SM = 1)
The Coleman-Weinberg mechanism is the sole source of symmetry breaking; no tree-level mass term exists
The high-temperature expansion of the finite-temperature effective potential is valid for the FOPT regime (supercooling regime with small field/T)
The Gaussian approximation for the false vacuum fraction is valid for computing the percolation temperature

backend_compatibility.notes: Model satisfies single-field conformal criterion: tree-level potential has only quartic term, masses proportional to single vev. The backend gb_scalar parameter is set to sqrt(beta_lambda) and is subleading.
